# EuConform

> Open infrastructure for AI Act evidence. Offline-first, structured, verifiable.

EuConform is an open-source evidence engine for European AI systems. It generates structured, machine-readable compliance evidence for the EU AI Act — without cloud dependencies, vendor lock-in, or opaque dashboards.

## Core Concept

EuConform scans real codebases and produces verifiable evidence bundles. The workflow is: scan → verify → view. Everything stays local and offline-first.

## The EuConform Evidence Format

The format is a protocol stack with four layers:

- **AI BOM** (`euconform.aibom.v1`): Machine-readable AI inventory — models, runtimes, providers, capabilities.
- **Report** (`euconform.report.v1`): Compliance signals, gaps, open questions, and recommendations for human review.
- **CI** (`euconform.ci.v1`): Lightweight enforcement for CI/CD pipelines — fail or warn on evidence thresholds.
- **Bundle** (`euconform.bundle.v1`): Verifiable manifest with SHA-256 hashes for portable, integrity-aware evidence transport.

## Key Facts

- License: Open source (MIT)
- Language: TypeScript
- CLI: `npx @euconform/cli scan ./your-project --zip`
- No cookies, no analytics, no tracking
- Designed for providers, deployers, importers, and distributors under the EU AI Act

## Links

- Website: https://euconform.eu
- GitHub: https://github.com/Hiepler/EuConform
- Specification: https://euconform.eu/spec
- German version: https://euconform.eu/de
